completion_promises.criterion_text: resume_value_as[T](typ: type[T], raw: T | dict | Any) -> T defined as a generic function
completion_promises.evidence: src/forge/adapters/langgraph/__init__.py defines `T = TypeVar('T', bound=BaseModel)` and `def resume_value_as(typ: type[T], raw: T | dict[str, Any] | Any) -> T:` — generic over any Pydantic model.

completion_promises.criterion_text: isinstance(raw, typ) short-circuit returns raw unchanged (no-op in direct-invoke mode)
completion_promises.evidence: First branch in resume_value_as: `if isinstance(raw, typ): return raw`. Verified by TestDirectInvokeShortCircuit.test_typed_input_is_returned_by_identity (asserts `result is original`, identity not equality) and test_typed_input_subclass_short_circuits.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Otherwise calls typ.model_validate(raw) (Pydantic v2) and returns the result
completion_promises.evidence: Second branch: `if isinstance(raw, dict): return typ.model_validate(raw)`. Verified by TestServerModeDictRehydration.test_dict_input_is_rehydrated_to_typed_instance and test_dict_input_with_invalid_field_raises_validation_error.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Raises a clear TypeError for inputs that are neither typ nor dict-like
completion_promises.evidence: Third branch raises TypeError with message including expected type, actual type, and bounded repr of raw. Verified by TestRejectsUnsupportedInputs parametrised across int/str/None/list/object — each asserts the message contains 'ApprovalResponsePayload' and 'dict'.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Group D Scenario Outline parametrised contract test: same caller code observes typed ApprovalResponsePayload whether input was typed or dict
completion_promises.evidence: test_group_d_scenario_outline_caller_code_is_mode_agnostic uses @pytest.mark.parametrize over (typed_direct_invoke, dict_server_mode) and asserts identical attribute access (.decision/.decided_by/.request_id) succeeds for both input shapes.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Direct-invoke test: typed input is returned unchanged (identity check, not just equality)
Server-mode test: dict input round-trips through model_validate and produces an equivalent typed instance
completion_promises.evidence: TestServerModeDictRehydration.test_dict_input_is_rehydrated_to_typed_instance constructs a dict payload, calls resume_value_as, asserts the result is an ApprovalResponsePayload with the same field values; test_dict_input_produces_new_instance_not_input verifies it is a fresh instance.

completion_promises.criterion_text: CI grep guard: scan forge/ for interrupt(...) followed by .decision/.responder attribute access in same function without intervening resume_value_as; fail test if found
completion_promises.evidence: test_rehydration_grep_guard_no_unhelpered_interrupt_attribute_access walks every src/forge/**/*.py via ast.parse, locates Call nodes with func name 'interrupt', flags Attribute nodes named .decision/.responder later in the same function unless an intervening resume_value_as Call exists or # noqa: rehydration-guard is on the line. Confidence tests (test_rehydration_grep_guard_detects_synthetic_violation, ...respects_allowlist_directive, ...accepts_helpered_access) prove the scanner detects, allow-lists, and accepts the helpered path correctly. AST-based scanning means docstring/comment mentions are ignored — only real code paths are inspected.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Module imports nothing from nats_core or nats-py (only langgraph types if needed)
completion_promises.evidence: TestModuleImportPurity.test_langgraph_adapter_does_not_import_nats_core uses ast.walk to inspect actual Import / ImportFrom nodes in src/forge/adapters/langgraph/__init__.py, asserting no import has root 'nats_core' or 'nats'. The module's only third-party import is `from pydantic import BaseModel`.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: Source follows PEP 8 / Black-compatible style: type hints, snake_case functions, max line width respected, docstrings on every public symbol. Module compiles without syntax errors (verified by ast.parse in the test) and the full non-BDD test suite passes (718 pre-existing tests + 17 new tests = 735 passing, 0 failures introduced). Project does not currently run an external linter in CI; the style conforms to .claude/rules/code-style.md.
